pi_items.source: Live environment REST API query: GET /patient?identifier=BAH000025&v=full returned display='BAH000025 - Grace Aiko Nakamura'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 28. Outside seed_bahmni.py range (BAH000002-BAH000021), so no collision with pre-seeded patients.

Live environment REST API: person.preferredName.givenName='Grace'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 29. Confirmed in live Clinical search UI screenshot (evidence 14_patient_in_clinical_search.png) showing 'Grace Nakamura BAH000025'.
Live environment REST API: person.preferredName.familyName='Nakamura'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 31. Confirmed in live patient dashboard screenshot showing 'Grace Nakamura (BAH000025)'.

Not in task.json metadata, but critical for task functionality. Live REST API: person.preferredName.middleName='Aiko'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 30. Required to prevent the 'Start OPD Visit' red-border validation failure observed in other Bahmni tasks (chronic_disease_followup).
Live REST API: person.gender='F'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 32. Confirmed in patient dashboard: 'Female, 58 years 6 months'.
Live REST API: person.birthdate='1967-09-14T00:00:00.000+0000'. Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 33. Patient dashboard shows '14 Sep 67' and age=58.

Live MySQL query on bahmni-openmrsdb: 8 Ciprofloxacin drug entries found including 'Ciprofloxacin 500 mg' (drug_id 163) and 'Ciprofloxacin 500mg Tablet' (drug_ids 518, 519). Agent can search and prescribe this drug.
Live REST API: GET /concept?q=Penicillin returned 10 results including 'Penicillin' (UUID 81724AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA). Agent can search for this when documenting allergy.
Live REST API: GET /concept?q=Appendicitis returned 'Appendicitis' (UUID 121518AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA) plus variants. Agent can select this for diagnosis.

Live REST API: GET /concept?q=Wound+Infection returned 'Wound Infection' (UUID f0ed3a4a-3814-4a7a-b824-f49825b9ca36) plus 'Postoperative wound infection' (113919). Agent can select either for diagnosis.
Live REST API: GET /concept?q=Complete+Blood+Count returned 'Complete blood count' (UUID 1019AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA) plus auto/manual variants. Agent can order this investigation.
Live REST API: GET /concept?q=C-Reactive+Protein returned 'C-reactive protein' (UUID 161500AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA) and 'C-reactive protein test' (161501). Agent can order this investigation.
Live MySQL query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM allergy WHERE patient_id=... AND voided=0 returned 0. No pre-existing allergies for BAH000025.
Live REST API: GET /order?patient={uuid}&t=drugorder returned 0 results. No pre-existing drug orders. Export_result.sh baseline correctly records 0.
Live MySQL query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM encounter_diagnosis ... returned 0. No pre-existing diagnoses for BAH000025.

Verified via live login: typing superman/Admin123 into the Bahmni login page at https://localhost/bahmni/home successfully authenticated and displayed the dashboard (evidence 13_bahmni_dashboard.png). Hardcoded in task_utils.sh openmrs_api_get/post functions.

Live run of export_result.sh produced valid JSON at this path with chmod 666 permissions. Confirmed in evidence 17_export_result_json.txt. All 7 scoring dimensions (vitals, allergy, medications, investigations, diagnoses, clinical_note, new_encounters) present with correct baseline values of 0/false.

Export result JSON shows vitals.count=0 despite 10 pre-seeded admission observations existing in the DB. The timestamp filter in export_result.sh (line 121: obs_dt < task_start_time) correctly excludes observations created 3 days before task start.

privileged_info_summary: Patient Grace Aiko Nakamura (BAH000025) is a 58-year-old female (DOB 1967-09-14) from 42 Riverside Drive, Nairobi Central, Kenya. She has an active OPD visit started 3 days prior. Pre-seeded admission vitals: Temperature 37.8C, Pulse 88, Systolic BP 126, Diastolic BP 78, Weight 64 kg (these are the HISTORICAL baseline values, NOT the values the agent must enter). The agent must enter NEW vitals: Temperature 38.4C, Pulse 96, BP 132/84 mmHg, Respiratory Rate 20, SpO2 97%, Weight 64 kg. The agent must document a Penicillin allergy (reaction: Urticaria, severity: Moderate) — the Penicillin concept exists in CIEL (UUID 81724AAA). The agent must prescribe Ciprofloxacin 500mg orally twice daily for 10 days — multiple Ciprofloxacin drug entries exist (drug_ids 162-165, 413-414, 518-519). The agent must NOT prescribe any Penicillin-class drug (amoxicillin, ampicillin, etc.). The agent must order Complete Blood Count (CIEL UUID 1019AAA) and C-Reactive Protein (UUID 161500AAA). The agent must record diagnoses Appendicitis (CIEL UUID 121518AAA, primary) and Wound Infection (UUID f0ed3a4a, secondary). The agent must write a clinical progress note of at least 200 characters. At baseline, the patient has 0 allergies, 0 drug orders, 0 test orders, and 0 diagnoses. The Bahmni allergy REST endpoint returns HTTP 500 on this version; allergy data is verified via MySQL direct query on the allergy table. The export script at /tmp/postop_infection_management_result.json captures all 7 dimensions with anti-gaming timestamp filtering that correctly excludes pre-seeded admission observations.
